_comment: Ledger of feedback submissions that have already been processed. Every row in the JGM readback should appear here so we don't act on it twice. Status is 'applied' (we made a change to the question bank) or 'ignored_duplicate' (the row reports a problem that was already fixed in an earlier commit; the user's PWA cache made them re-report it).

Reworded prompt to lead with Yushchenko as PM; added Kuchma-as-president context and Tymoshenko's energy portfolio to the explanation. Submitted without *JGM* tag but confirmed by JGM as his own feedback.

Removed 'The lectures contrast...' framing from explanation; reworded as a direct statement of the China/USSR contrast.

Expanded 'Ofer' to 'Gur Ofer' in the correct choice and added an explanation sentence identifying him (Hebrew University economist, 1987 JEL survey 'Soviet Economic Growth: 1928-1985') so the reference is not opaque.

Per JGM: kept 'why' framing but rewrote all three distractors as alternative causal explanations (oil/gas rents, 1998-weakened governor corps, 2004 constitutional devolution) rather than premise-contradicting claims; expanded explanation to mention 2004 gubernatorial-election abolition and United Russia.
Clarified 'White House' as the Moscow building housing the Supreme Soviet in both the correct choice and the explanation, to avoid confusion with the US executive mansion.

Removed 'JGM's lectures single out', 'the course's account', 'in the lectures', and 'in this course' phrasing from explanation and distractor notes. Also triggered a full-bank sweep (see next ledger entry).
Shortened the correct-answer option (~90 words → ~50) and trimmed the explanation to reduce length-mismatch across the four choices per JGM.

Replaced 'December 1994 invasion' framing (awkward since Russia did not recognize Chechen independence) with 'Russian federal forces moved to reassert control' in both the correct choice and explanation.
Difficulty raised from medium to hard per JGM.

Removed 'the course uses' from explanation. This submission and 785e0e6c both asked for a full-bank sweep; also fixed 13 other questions with similar 'the lectures/course/JGM's lecture/handout' phrasing: et_0037, et_0052, et_0059, pt_0012, pt_0137, pt_0138, pt_0139, pt_0140, pt_0142, pt_0144, pt_0145, fp_0131, pt_0148.

Replaced the logically-inconsistent 'mismatch impossible' distractor with an alternative-cause distractor (post-1991 Russian labor migration into eastern Ukraine), per JGM rule 15. Updated note to explain why the new distractor is wrong (1989 census predates 1991 migration).
Cut question entirely from the bank and from proposed_questions_batch5.json per JGM: 'super easy and not directly tied to anything in the post Soviet region'. Bank now 408.
Replaced 'roughly 250 deputies' with the exact count: 252 (Council of the Republic + Council of Nationalities, 126 each).
Trimmed correct option per rule 14 (Matej flagged length asymmetry); moved IMF/hryvnia/inflation specifics to explanation.
Made Russia the explicit subject in correct option ('Russia's own privileged interests in neighboring regions') per Matej.
Matej asked 'Privileged or Privilege?' — the correct option already used 'Privileged' (correct adjective form). No edit needed.
Replaced 'halfway state' with Hellman's actual term 'partial-reform equilibrium' in the correct option, per Matej.
Reworded correct option to be more descriptive (concentrated vs. broad ownership) instead of evaluative ('notably fairer system'), per Matej's normative-tone observation.
Trimmed correct option (removed Russian-term parentheticals like 'kolkhozy/sovkhozy'); kept the gloss in the explanation. Per Matej + rule 14.
Glossed 'power vertical' inline with the Russian term (vertikal vlasti) and a one-clause definition, per Matej.
Replaced ambiguous 'They' with explicit 'The 1989 elections' in all four answer options, per Matej.
Replaced ambiguous 'republican autonomy' with 'autonomy gains made by the Soviet republics' in correct option, per Matej.
Trimmed correct option per rule 14; Matej called out length asymmetry from his SAT-development experience.
Glossed Kuchma in the explanation ('Ukraine's president since 1994') so students don't need to know his role from prior context, per Matej.
Expanded explanation to gloss 'Orange Camp' on first mention (Yushchenko's Our Ukraine + Tymoshenko's bloc, post-2004 Orange Revolution), per Matej.
